title: Launch Academy profile ties together Ph.D., Beckman, and Canada rationale
excerpt: The 2018 Launch Academy / Maple profile is one of the most useful public documents because it links Sean Xiang's CUHK doctorate, Beckman Laser Institute role, and the strategic case for expanding into Canada.
caption: This functions less like decorative press coverage and more like a connective archival document between the academic chapter and later geographic expansion.

title: OpenStack community release is the earliest strong public company anchor
excerpt: The February 2013 OpenStack announcement is more than an ecosystem badge. It publicly names Sean Xiang as co-founder and CEO while positioning Bloombase around enterprise cloud privacy, compliance, and data-at-rest protection.
caption: Instead of pretending to have exact incorporation paperwork, the chronicle can use this dated release as the first hard public proof that the founder chapter was already legible in market-facing language.
visualText: 2013-02-18 · Sean Xiang is quoted as co-founder and CEO while the product is framed as the missing security layer for OpenStack storage.
quoteText: One of the key challenges with enterprise core business applications on cloud adoption is the need to achieve data privacy and regulatory compliance.

title: The VMware alliance shows Bloombase entering enterprise infrastructure fast
excerpt: The August 2013 VMware Technology Alliance announcement tightens the founding chapter because it shows Bloombase speaking directly to virtualization and cloud buyers within months of the earliest visible 2013 public trail.
caption: Editorially, this is the right kind of founding-era artifact: concrete, dated, and structurally aligned with the long-term thesis that Bloombase was built inside trust-heavy infrastructure markets from the beginning.
visualText: 2013-08-30 · A direct company URL ties Bloombase to VMware's alliance program and to virtualization-era data privacy concerns.
quoteText: Data privacy tops one of CIOs' concerns over virtualization and cloud.

title: VMware alliance marks the start of a visible enterprise-validation run
excerpt: The January 2014 Bloombase announcement about joining VMware's Technology Alliance Partner Program gives the middle chapter a concrete starting marker in platform interoperability and ecosystem trust.
caption: One announcement is not the whole story, but it helps establish the cadence of repeated validation signals that follow.

title: VMware Cloud on AWS shows the company translating into the cloud stack
excerpt: The 2018 StoreSafe for VMware Cloud on AWS announcement matters because it shows the validation story evolving beyond on-prem credibility into a cloud-era deployment surface.
caption: This is the kind of artifact that keeps the middle chapter from reading like a static partner list: the platform context keeps changing, and Bloombase keeps trying to stay legible inside it.

title: The NVIDIA DPU / GPU post reframes Bloombase for accelerated computing
excerpt: Bloombase's 2023 post on rebuilding data-at-rest security with NVIDIA DPUs and GPUs is the clearest public signal that the company is trying to translate long-built security primitives into an AI-era stack.
caption: Editorially, this is the bridge between legacy enterprise credibility and present-tense AI relevance.

title: The Utimaco alliance gives the AI-era chapter a direct primary source
excerpt: The April 2025 Bloombase announcement explicitly frames the Utimaco relationship around data security solutions for the AI era, making it a stronger anchor than generic conference visibility alone.
caption: This is the kind of source that can justify richer future media treatment because it is direct, dated, and specific.
